Title: Create a new Person
Preconditions: None
Test Steps:
1. Call the `create()` method with the name "John Doe"
2. Verify that the `update()` method cannot be called before `create()`
Expected Result:
- The `create()` method successfully creates a new person with the name "John Doe"
- The `update()` method throws an exception or returns an error indicating that the person does not exist yet

Title: Update an existing Person
Preconditions: A person has been created using the `create()` method
Test Steps:
1. Call the `update()` method with the name "Jane Smith"
2. Verify that the `delete()` method cannot be called before `update()`
Expected Result:
- The `update()` method successfully updates the person's name to "Jane Smith"
- The `delete()` method throws an exception or returns an error indicating that the person does not exist yet

Title: Delete an existing Person
Preconditions: A person has been updated using the `update()` method
Test Steps:
1. Call the `delete()` method
2. Verify that the `create()` method cannot be called after `delete()`
Expected Result:
- The `delete()` method successfully deletes the person
- The `create()` method throws an exception or returns an error indicating that the person does not exist anymore

Preconditions: No person has been created
Test Steps: 1. Call the `update()` method with the name "Alice Johnson"
Expected Result: - The `update()` method throws an exception or returns an error indicating that the person does not exist

Title: Attempt to delete a non-existent Person
Preconditions: No person has been created
Test Steps: 1. Call the `delete()` method
Expected Result: - The `delete()` method throws an exception or returns an error indicating that the person does not exist
